title: Mediterranean Climate Patterns
extract: The Mediterranean climate, classified under the Köppen system as Csa and Csb, is characterized by warm to hot, dry summers and mild, wet winters. This climate type occurs primarily between 30° and 45° latitude on the western sides of continents. In Europe, the Mediterranean basin supports unique biodiversity, including approximately 25,000 plant species, roughly half of which are endemic. Agricultural systems adapted to this climate have produced distinctive cultural landscapes, from the terraced vineyards of southern France to the olive groves of Greece and the citrus orchards of Spain.
